articles.title: Woodside Denies Exxon Deal Talks Amid Takeover Speculation
articles.summary: Woodside said it is not aware of any proposal from Exxon Mobil and is not engaged in takeover discussions.

articles.title: Woodside moves to acquire PetroChina’s 10.67% Browse JV stake
articles.summary: Under the terms of the acquisition, Woodside will pay CNPC $225m (A$319.13m) for the interest.

articles.title: Woodside Boosts Browse Stake by Exercising Pre-Emption Rights
articles.summary: Woodside has exercised its pre-emption rights to acquire PetroChina's 10.67% stake in the Browse Joint Venture, increasing its interest in the project to 41.27%.

articles.title: Fluor JV receives LNTP for LNG Canada phase two expansion
articles.summary: LNG Canada is jointly owned by Shell, Petronas, PetroChina, Mitsubishi and KOGAS.

articles.title: BP Sells 5% Browse Stake As Asian LNG Supply Shifts
articles.summary: BP reduces its Browse stake while Asian buyers increase exposure to Australian LNG supply.

articles.title: Petro Matad expects PetroChina greenlight 'shortly'
articles.summary: Petro Matad Limited (AIM:MATD, OTC:PRTDF, FRA:HA3) told investors that the approval of its 2026 Oil Sales Agreement with PetroChina is expected shortly, potentially unlocking sales of around 35,000 barrels of Block XX crude accumulated so far this year. articles.title: Asian Dividend Stocks To Enhance Your Portfolio
articles.summary: As Asian markets navigate a landscape marked by geopolitical tensions and evolving economic indicators, investors are increasingly turning their attention to stable income-generating opportunities. In this context, dividend stocks in Asia offer a compelling proposition for those seeking to enhance their portfolios with reliable returns amidst market uncertainties.

articles.title: CATL Surges To $306 Billion Valuation, Overtakes PetroChina Again
articles.summary: Battery maker's profit beat and storage momentum lift shares as oil prices ease on ceasefire talks

articles.title: PetroChina Balances Softer Earnings With Output Growth And Dividend Plans
articles.summary: PetroChina (SEHK:857) released its latest full year results, showing resilient performance despite a pullback in annual earnings linked to weaker oil prices and softer fuel demand. The company reported strong margins and substantial oil and gas output, along with major discoveries in key Chinese basins. Management also highlighted progress on low carbon initiatives and international projects, describing these as part of a broader long term transformation. PetroChina is one of China’s...

articles.title: PetroChina Profit Falls to 157.3B Yuan as Brent Drops 15%
articles.summary: Lower crude prices and weaker fuel demand weigh on earnings, while natural gas growth and higher refining profits provide partial support.
